user_query:
I'm trying to align our team docs with what's actually in the projects and what our last build attempt reported. Using only the files in this repo, please do the following and keep everything self-contained:

1) Parse the project files at:
- src/App/App.csproj
- src/Lib/Lib.csproj

Then generate a single JSON report at output/dependency-report.json with this top-level shape:
{
  "projects": [
    { "name": string, "path": string, "targetFramework": string, "packages": [ { "name": string, "version": string } ] }
  ],
  "conflicts": [
    { "package": string, "versionsByProject": { [projectName]: version } }
  ],
  "frameworks": [string],
  "issues": {
    "errors": [ { "code": string, "count": number, "sampleMessage": string } ],
    "warnings": [ { "code": string, "count": number, "sampleMessage": string } ]
  }
}

Rules for the report:
- Extract each project's TargetFramework and all PackageReference names/versions from the csproj XML.
- A conflict is any package that appears with different versions across projects; include all versions keyed by project name in versionsByProject.
- frameworks is the set of unique TargetFramework values across projects.
- For issues, analyze input/build_log.txt and group by code and severity. Count occurrences of lines that start with "error CODE" or "warning CODE" (case-insensitive). sampleMessage should be the first line text for that code as it appears in the log (trimmed, without following detail lines).

2) Update docs/CONTRIBUTING.md in place to reflect the current state:
- Replace any single "Target Framework:" line with a "Target Frameworks" section that lists each project with its target framework, e.g., "App: netX.Y" and "Lib: netA.B" based on the csproj files.
- Replace the existing "Known issues" section with a bullet list of the error and warning codes found in input/build_log.txt, each including the total count in parentheses, e.g., "- error NETSDK1045 (1)", "- warning CS1591 (2)". Keep the rest of the file unchanged.
- Keep the existing restore/build command wording but add a brief note under Restore mentioning if any cross-project package version conflicts were detected (based on the conflicts in output/dependency-report.json), naming the affected packages.

Inputs to use:
- src/App/App.csproj
- src/Lib/Lib.csproj
- input/build_log.txt
- docs/CONTRIBUTING.md

Deliverables:
- output/dependency-report.json (created or overwritten with the schema above)
- docs/CONTRIBUTING.md (edited in place to include the updated Target Frameworks and Known issues)

Please do not introduce any new external dependencies or fetch anything from the internet; rely only on the provided files and produce the outputs exactly at the paths listed. All file paths are relative to the workspace directory.
